ttl.name: ENGINE IMMOBILISER SYSTEM (w/ Smart Key System)

item.ptxt: ID codes are the same as recognition codes for the wireless transmitter and the engine immobiliser function. Registering an ID code enables the smart access system with push-button start, the wireless door lock control function and the engine immobiliser function to be operated.
Code registration is needed when the certification ECU, ID code box, steering lock ECU or key is replaced with a new one.

s2.ptxt: The vehicle with the smart access system with push-button start system does not have a key slot. Therefore, hold the key close to the engine switch to register the key, as shown in the illustration below.
The following table shows ECU replacement and key registration procedures in case the malfunctioning ECU has been identified after troubleshooting the smart access system with push-button start.

item.ptxt: The following procedures indicated in the table below require the use of the intelligent tester:
New key ID registration
Additional key ID registration
Key ID erasure
ECU code registration
If all of the registered keys are not available, replacement of the ID code box is also required.
A maximum of 7 keys can be registered.

entry.ptxt: 2. Reregister all keys (new key ID registration)
Key ID codes can be registered and erased
2. Perform additional key registration procedure (additional key ID registration)
4. Reregister all keys (new key ID registration)

atten4.ptxt: If some keys are not registered during above steps, they will be disabled because they cannot be registered later

entry.ptxt: Key ID codes cannot be either registered or erased
3. Reregister all keys (new key ID registration)

atten4.ptxt: If key codes cannot be erased or additional keys cannot be registered due to a malfunction in certification ECU, replace ID code box and certification ECU. If some keys are not registered during above steps, they will be disabled because they cannot be registered later

entry.ptxt: 2. Register recognition codes in ECUs (ECU code registration)
3. Reregister all keys (new key ID registration)

atten4.ptxt: If some keys are not registered during above steps, they will be disabled because they cannot be registered later

entry.ptxt: Customer has brought at least 1 key
2. Register recognition codes in ECUs (ECU code registration)
4. Reregister all keys (new key ID registration)

atten4.ptxt: If some keys are not registered during above steps, they will be disabled because they cannot be registered later

entry.ptxt: Customer has brought at least 1 key
1. Using remaining key, erase lost key code (key ID erasure)
2. Register additional keys as necessary (additional key ID registration)
3. Register all keys (new key ID registration)

atten4.ptxt: If customer brings lost keys at later date, they can be registered using additional key ID registration function

s2.ptxt: New key ID registration (when replacing certification ECU and ID code box, or certification ECU, ID code box and steering lock ECU)

item.ptxt: Connect intelligent tester (with CAN VIM) to DLC3
Turn engine switch on (IG)
Select "SMART ACCESS / ID UTILITY / SMART CODE REG" from tester menu

atten4.ptxt: The engine switch cannot be turned on (IG) more than 10 times. After connecting tester, turn tester on while turning driver's side door courtesy light switch on and off repeatedly at 1.5-second intervals or less to continue key registration procedure

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen

atten4.ptxt: The mode is automatically selected by tester, new registration mode or add mode

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds. Place unregistered key on front passenger's side seat
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Finish new key ID code registration

*1: Repeat this process for each key which is to be registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure for each key within 30 seconds. If the procedure for any of the keys has not been finished within the specified time, perform registration procedures from process 1 again. Make sure that only 1 key is in the cabin during registration procedures. If 2 or more keys are in the cabin simultaneously, electric waves will interfere with each other, preventing normal registration.

s2.ptxt: New key ID registration (when replacing certification ECU)

item.ptxt: Connect intelligent tester (with CAN VIM) to DLC3
Turn engine switch on (IG)
Select "SMART ACCESS / ID UTILITY / SMART CODE REG" from tester menu

atten4.ptxt: The engine switch cannot be turned on (IG) more than 10 times. After connecting tester, turn tester on while turning driver's side door courtesy light switch on and off repeatedly at 1.5-second intervals or less to continue key registration procedure

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen

atten4.ptxt: The mode is automatically selected by tester, new registration mode or add mode

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds. Place unregistered key on front passenger side seat
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Finish new key ID code registration

*1: Repeat this process for each key registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure for each key within 30 seconds. If the procedure for any of the keys has not been finished within the specified time, perform registration procedures from process 1 again. If performing the key confirmation procedure for a key, the security indicator comes on and remains on until all the keys are confirmed.

*2: Repeat this process for each key which is to be registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure for each key within 30 seconds. If the procedure for any of the keys has not been finished within the specified time, perform registration procedures from 1 again. Make sure that only 1 key is in the cabin during registration procedures. If 2 or more keys are in the cabin simultaneously, electric waves will interfere with each other, preventing normal registration.
Connect intelligent tester (with CAN VIM) to DLC3
Turn engine switch on (IG)
Select "SMART ACCESS / ID UTILITY / SMART CODE REG" from tester menu

entry.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on intelligent tester screen

atten4.ptxt: The mode is automatically selected by tester, new registration mode or add mode

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds once

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds. Place unregistered key on front passenger's side seat
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Finish key ID code registration

*1: Perform this process for one of the keys registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure within 30 seconds. If the procedure has not been finished within the specified time, perform registration procedures from process 1 again.

*2: Repeat this process for each key which is to be registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure for each key within 30 seconds. If the procedure for any of the keys has not been finished within the specified time, perform registration procedures from 1 again. Make sure that only 1 key is in the cabin during registration procedures. If 2 or more keys are in the cabin simultaneously, electric waves will interfere with each other, preventing normal registration.

atten4.ptxt: Erase all registered key codes except one.

item.ptxt: Connect intelligent tester (with CAN VIM) to DLC3
Turn engine switch on (IG)
Select "SMART ACCESS / ID UTILITY / SMART CODE ERS" from tester menu

entry.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on intelligent tester screen
Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds once

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Finish key ID code erasure

*1: Perform this process for one of the keys registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure within 30 seconds. If the procedure has not been finished within the specified time, perform erasure procedures from process 1 again.
Connect intelligent tester (with CAN VIM) to DLC3
Turn engine switch on (IG)
Select "SMART ACCESS / ID UTILITY / ECU COMM ID REG / ID Code Box and Steering Lock" from tester menu

entry.ptxt: Hold unregistered key close to engine switch (For details, refer to PROCEDURE "1")
Confirm that wireless door lock buzzer sounds once

item.ptxt: Perform operation according to prompts on tester screen
Finish ECU code registration

*1: Perform this process for one of the keys registered for the vehicle. Finish the procedure within 30 seconds. If the procedure has not been finished within the specified time, perform erasure procedures from process 1 again.
The ECU communication ID should be registered when the ID code box and/or the ECM is replaced in order to match these ECM communication ID.
The engine cannot be started unless the ECM communication ID matches.

s3.ptxt: Using SST, connect terminals TC and CG of the DLC3.
Turn the engine switch on (IG) and leave it for 30 minutes.
Turn the engine switch off and disconnect terminals TC and CG.
